threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the buses don’t stop at the actual bus stops. Local wisdom: it’s all about the wave.
Saw a guy on the 77 last night singing to his reflection in the window. Clapham lights make for a pretty good stage, I guess.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using the U-Bahn as their personal skateboard park during the strike—only in Neukölln would that feel normal.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know it’s Berlin when the bus driver stops for a minute to let a group of free-roaming cats cross the street. Only here, right?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they know to avoid 16th and Mission during rush hour. Only tourists linger there, oblivious to the chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 31 bus playing chess with himself. He had a full set and was deep in thought, completely oblivious to the chaos around him.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think paella is a daily staple here. In Gràcia, you're more likely to find people digging into a good 'bocadillo' or 'escalivada' instead.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their reluctance to try anything but paella; a true local knows it's all about the xató and calçots.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'the Tube' is the only way to get around. Meanwhile, locals are all about the buses, especially the 38.
Caught the 103 late last night, and the driver serenaded us with old soul classics. Never knew a bus could feel like home.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a busker playing accordion to a crowd of joggers at Tempelhofer Feld. That blend of chaos and calm felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous dance parties on the U-Bahn platforms, where commuters transform into dancers at a moment's notice. Pure Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their old-school preference for the 14 over the new train lines. We know the 14 has soul, even when it’s late.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 this morning, driver offered a doughnut to a regular who forgot his fare. Just another day in the city, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the quiet plazas where locals sip cafès and gossip. They miss the heart.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they ask for directions to Parc Güell—like it’s the only place they’ve heard of.
